tracks.meaning: a device for making or breaking an electrical connection or changing the direction of an electric current
to change from one thing to another or exchange positions
to change the topic of conversation or redirect attention
a control mechanism to toggle between different states, modes, or programs
to change direction or reverse course, especially suddenly
to shift one's attention, focus, or emotional state from one thing to another
a punishment in sports where a player is sent to the penalty box or removed from play
a thin stick or rod used to hit something, particularly a riding crop or cane
a sudden reversal in fortune or a dramatic change in plot
to exchange one thing for another in a fraudulent manner or bait-and-switch tactic
a portable electrical device that controls the ignition or detonation of an explosive
to change the course of a sailing ship by turning it across the wind
a thin flexible rod or whip used to drive cattle or animals
